parsed_review.summary: CP00-408 is a descriptor-only payments-core (RP13) closeout/foundation pack covering 150 planned units (RP13.P06.M06.S07-RP13.P07.M03.S06) that closes RP13.P06 (M06-M10) and opens the RP13.P07 foundation (M00-M03 head). The pack-level claim is supported and not over-stated: production_ready=false in both command-evidence.json and manifest.json, with the Hermes packet asserting only a gated production_ready_candidate (coverage.valid && slice.valid). Cross-surface consistency is strong — the registry binding, CP408 requirements distributions (deliverable/phase/micro-phase/micro-title, all summing to 150), the 9 required section row-sets, the 7 required public exports, the contract current_pack (deep-equal asserted), the closeout-pack-plan live cursor, the validator script, and three unit tests all agree. Descriptor-only/no-write/no-real-data boundaries are comprehensively attested at row, factory, attestation, and manifest levels (no payments/matching/AR/journal runtime, no state writes, no Hermes runtime receipt, no leaks, deny-over-allow). Authority boundary is preserved: Claude is read-only (tools Read/Grep/Glob), not final approver (claude_is_final_approval=false, promotes_claude_to_final_approval=false), enterprise trust not claimed from local validation, and human final approval gates runtime opening (deferred to CP00-409). Hermes/control-plane patterns are confined to the deterministic review packet/receipt sequence (baseline→readiness→read-only run→normalize→validate→adjudicate→inspect→closeout→commit), not product runtime. The CP178+ hardening blocklist (failed auth, zero-byte, malformed JSON, fenced JSON, tool-call-shaped output, disabled tools, missing raw-output/source-inspection basis, final-approval/source-mutation/enterprise-trust claims) is wired into the Claude packet, and the auth preflight passed with valid non-empty JSON. Changed-file scope correctly isolates the 8 implementation refs and preserves unrelated dirty files (LDIP result, workbook docs, docx) as out-of-pack. One non-blocking informational note: the manifest references post-review evidence artifacts not yet present, which is the expected pre-commit state produced by the hardened review sequence.

findings.title: Manifest evidence_refs point to post-review artifacts not yet generated (expected pre-review state)
findings.details: manifest.evidence_refs lists docs/closeout-packs/cp00-408/claude-review-result.json, adjudication.md, and construction-inspection.json, none of which exist yet in that directory (only manifest.json and command-evidence.json are present). This is not a defect: command-evidence.json explicitly documents that the post-review gated commands require a claude-review-result.json 'produced from the accepted review' and are 'executed and recorded before commit', and the hardened_review_sequence (packages/payments/src/registry.js:613) orders read_only_claude_run_with_raw_capture -> receipt_normalization -> receipt_validation -> finding_adjudication -> construction_inspection -> final_closeout_validation -> commit. The current review run is the step that produces the review result; the named artifacts are generated downstream. No over-claim is present (command-evidence production_ready=false, manifest production_ready=false, review_hardening.invalid_attempts_preserved=[]).
findings.recommendation: No code change required. Ensure the three post-review artifacts (claude-review-result.json, adjudication.md, construction-inspection.json) are generated from this accepted review and pass final-closeout validation (npm run closeout-pack:validate and receipt validation) before commit, as already gated by the hardened review sequence.
findings.post_review_disposition: Informational only; expected pre-review/pre-commit state gated by the deterministic hardened review sequence. Non-blocking; does not affect pack or goal closeout.
